scenario: A team's BPMN files live in a Git repo. They want bidirectional sync between their Web Modeler project and Git -- pushes from Modeler land in Git, and Git changes appear in Modeler.
question: Which Web Modeler feature fits?

options.text: Web Modeler Git sync -- configured on a Process Application (not a raw project folder). Org owners and administrators set up a Git connection supporting GitHub, GitLab, Azure DevOps, Bitbucket Cloud, and Bitbucket Data Center. Editors push changes from Web Modeler to Git and pull external commits using the built-in sync button. Changes from both directions are reconciled, enabling a true bidirectional Git workflow.

Manual workflow: export BPMN files from Web Modeler as a ZIP archive, then manually commit and push to the Git repository. This approach requires human intervention at each step -- exporting, unzipping, committing, and pushing. Changes made directly in Git must be re-imported manually. The built-in Git sync feature eliminates all these manual steps by providing automated bidirectional synchronisation integrated into the Web Modeler UI.

Web Modeler has no Git integration capability and operates as a closed-loop tool disconnected from external version control systems. This is factually incorrect. Web Modeler includes a first-class Git sync feature supporting major Git providers. Org administrators can connect a Process Application to a Git repository, enabling bidirectional synchronisation of BPMN, DMN, and Form files between Web Modeler and the configured version control system.

Switch to Desktop Modeler which natively integrates with Git, instead of using Web Modeler. Desktop Modeler is a locally installed client application that works with local file systems where users can manage their own Git commits externally using standard tools. The question specifically concerns Web Modeler's built-in Git sync feature, which provides native bidirectional synchronisation without switching to a different modelling application.

a.text: Correct. Web Modeler offers Git/GitHub sync -- configure the connection at the project level; commits flow both ways.

d.text: Incorrect. Web Modeler is the question's tool; switching defeats the workflow.

explanation: Web Modeler offers Git/GitHub sync -- configure the connection at the project level; commits flow both ways.
